capability_objective: Show the active shipment records at Portland Hub. Use the structured status-and-location filters rather than text search, and do not inspect records elsewhere or make any changes.
prompt:
The outbound coordination desk needs a read-only snapshot. Work only in the provided sandbox and use the genuine `./shipping-registry` executable documented in `README.md` through Pi's Bash tool. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing data, or its audit evidence. Do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under `.harness` or `.shipping`.

Show the active shipment records at Portland Hub. Choose the single registry operation intended for structured status-and-location filtering and invoke exactly one successful registry operation. Use both structured filters in that operation. Do not use text search, retrieve records individually, list without both filters, inspect records at any other location or status, read the profile, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not alter registry data, audit evidence, or protected verification files, and do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete audit events.

Write the result to `active_shipments.md` and no other new file. The file must contain a Markdown table with exactly this header and divider: `| ID | Name | Location | Status | Date |` and `| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |`. Include one row for every record returned by the structured operation, sorted by ID ascending, preserving all five values exactly as returned. Add no heading, prose, blank line, or unrelated record, and end the file with one newline. After writing the file, return exactly the same table as the final response.
